meaning: n.鍛戒护,鎸囦护,[寰媇绂佷护(娉曢櫌寮哄埗琚憡浠庝簨鎴栦笉寰椾粠浜嬫煇椤硅涓虹殑姝ｅ紡鍛戒护)
examples: The court granted an injunction against the defendants.
They have obtained an injunction restraining the company from selling the product.
He took out a court injunction against the newspaper demanding the return of the document.
It wasted no time in getting an injunction that sent Hug to the sidelines for six months.
"They want an injunction" to shut Ditto down, says Ms. Endress, 30 years old.
